definition: A person who competes for a title, prize, or position; one who has a realistic chance of winning.
example: Every champion was once a contender who refused to give up.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: Steady persistence in a course of action, a purpose, or a state, especially in spite of difficulties, obstacles, or discouragement.
example: Perseverance is going one more round when you don’t think you can—that’s what makes all the difference.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: The capacity to recover quickly from difficulties; toughness; the ability to spring back into shape.
example: Resilience isn’t how hard you hit; it’s how hard you can get hit and keep moving forward.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: Courage and resolve; strength of character; firmness of mind or spirit.
example: Grit faces a mean world: nobody hits as hard as life, but you keep moving forward anyway.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: Courage, spirit, or enthusiasm; the emotional or moral core that drives determination and passion.
example: Heart isn’t about how hard you hit—it’s about how hard you can get hit and keep moving forward.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: A competitor thought to have little chance of winning a fight or contest; one who is expected to lose.
example: The underdog may not beat him, but can go the distance—and know for the first time he isn’t just another bum.

definition: Difficulties; misfortune; a state of hardship or affliction.
example: Adversity fills a mean, nasty world; it doesn’t care how tough you are—so you move forward anyway.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: A person who has defeated or surpassed all rivals in a competition; one who fights or argues for a cause or on behalf of others.
example: Every champion was once a contender who refused to give up.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.

definition: The full length of a race or struggle; endurance to the finish.
example: I can’t beat him, but I can go the distance—and that will mean I am not just another bum from the neighborhood.

definition: Toward a point ahead; onward in progress.
example: It is not how hard you hit—it is how hard you can get hit and keep moving forward.

definition: Trust, faith, or confidence in someone or something.
example: Belief is a muscle: unused, it atrophies under other people’s opinions.

definition: A unit of play in a contest; another attempt after fatigue.
example: Going in one more round when you don’t think you can—that is perseverance with a mouthguard.

definition: Strong enough to withstand adverse conditions; resilient.
example: Life is tough; tough is not the same as cruel—keep your heart while you take the punches.

definition: A district or community within a town or city.
example: He fought so the neighborhood would see that somebody from these streets could stand tall.

definition: The faculty by which a person decides on and initiates action.
example: Talent opens the door; will keeps you in the gym after talent gets tired.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.
speech: Rocky to his son Rocky Jr.
